title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault after allegedly beating a 28-year-old man near Winter Park.
Victim was tied with an extension cord, bleeding, and taken to a nearby residence; motive unclear.
Suspects include Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, all scheduled for arraignment Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales, 32, and David Ardila, 33, were arrested at Logan Airport for immigration issues.
Rosales' pending DACA renewal and Ardila's overstayed visa are central to their arrests.
Their attorney reports an increase in Logan Airport immigration-related arrests, mostly of individuals with no criminal history.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Interstellar' (2014) is recommended for its parallels to Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' in terms of epic storytelling.
'The Searchers' (1956) reflects thematic similarities to 'The Odyssey' with a journey motif in John Ford's Western classic.
'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' (2004) offers a humorous take on 'The Odyssey' through underwater adventures and mythical parallels.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games by sweeping Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Jarren Duran and Carlos Narvaez drive in runs during a 10-0 victory in Game 1.
Boston secures 5-3 win in Game 2 with bullpen allowing zero runs and four hits.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early may soon return from injuries; Suarez could start on July 21.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo show progress in rehab but have no specific return dates.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others remain shut down with no updates or timetable for return.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida shined with three hits, including a home run, leading to Boston's success in Game 1 against the Rays.
Caleb Durbin contributed with five hits across both doubleheader games, consistently reaching base to challenge Tampa Bay's staff.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings in Game 1, aiding Boston’s bullpen strategy for the doubleheader sweep.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft voiced support for coach Mike Vrabel following controversy involving NFL reporter Dianna Russini.
Vrabel denied affair claims with Russini, who resigned from The Athletic amid an investigation into their interactions.
New England Patriots training camp begins next Saturday as the team prepares to improve under head coach Mike Vrabel.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Lucky' starring Anya Taylor-Joy is now streaming on Apple TV, exploring family crime dynamics.
'Obsession,' a 2026 horror hit on Peacock, grossed over $400 million from a $750,000 budget.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' featuring Sam Neill, is streaming on Prime Video and Plex.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin was ruled to have violated the NH Civil Rights Act in a 2024 attack on a transgender woman.
Lufkin attacked the victim, using a slur, at a Concord Speedway store; he fled on a bicycle after the assault.
Lufkin received a $5,000 civil fine, and a 12-month sentence, for second-degree and simple assault convictions.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox chief baseball officer Craig Breslow remains undecided on trade deadline strategy as team considers adding players.
Boston's recent 10-game winning streak raises playoff odds to 44.3% but plans remain flexible before August 3 deadline.
MLB insider reports suggest trading closer Aroldis Chapman is unlikely, despite potential value if Red Sox choose to sell.
